arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import imaplib
import email
from email.header import decode_header

mail = imaplib.IMAP4_SSL('imap.gmail.com')
mail.login('bullsagetrader@gmail.com', 'ncdu eehe vbqo nznm')
mail.select('inbox')

# Search for API-related emails
keywords = ['finnhub', 'alpha vantage', 'alphavantage', 'coingecko', 'twelve data', 'twelvedata', 'financial modeling', 'notion', 'api key', 'welcome', 'account']
all_ids = set()
for kw in keywords:
    status, data = mail.search(None, f'(OR SUBJECT "{kw}" FROM "{kw}")')
    if status == 'OK' and data[0]:
        all_ids.update(data[0].split())

print(f"Found {len(all_ids)} relevant emails\n")

for eid in sorted(all_ids, key=lambda x: int(x)):
    status, msg_data = mail.fetch(eid, '(RFC822)')
    if status != 'OK':
        continue
    msg = email.message_from_bytes(msg_data[0][1])
    
    subj = decode_header(msg['Subject'])[0]
    subject = subj[0].decode(subj[1] or 'utf-8') if isinstance(subj[0], bytes) else subj[0]
    from_ = msg['From']
    date_ = msg['Date']
    
    # Get body
    body = ''
    if msg.is_multipart():
        for part in msg.walk():
            if part.get_content_type() == 'text/plain':
                body = part.get_payload(decode=True).decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
                break
    else:
        body = msg.get_payload(decode=True).decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
    
    print(f"=== FROM: {from_}")
    print(f"    SUBJ: {subject}")
    print(f"    DATE: {date_}")
    # Print first 500 chars of body to find API keys
    print(f"    BODY: {body[:800]}")
    print()

mail.logout()
EOF

OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Welcome-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Hi bull,

Welcome to OpenRouter - the world's largest gateway for artificial intelligence.

Use them directly, or use your OpenRouter API key and plug it into these apps to keep all your LLM usage under one account.
* Use our API to build your own apps and agents - get started here ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/quickstart?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Welcome-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

Hi bull,

Providers have outages. It's just the nature of the business.

When a provider fails, OpenRouter continues to serve your users by routing requests to an alternative provider, at no extra cost.

You can learn more about uptime optimization here ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/best-practices/uptime-optimization?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Routing-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

Subject: Protecting your data: logging and training policies
OpenRouter

OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Logging-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Hi bull,

OpenRouter never logs your prompts or responses by default.
-----------------------------------------------------------

We only store metadata to keep your service running smoothly:

* Token counts and latency for billing
* Performance metrics for uptime optimization
* Generation activity you can access anytime

You control exactly where your data ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/privacy?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Logging-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) goes.

Each provider has their own data retention and training policies, and you can check a straightforward summary here ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/privacy-logging?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Logging-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io#data-retention--logging ). 

By default, your paid requests never route to providers that train on your data. You can customize this further by:

* Setting data policies per request in your API calls
* Configuring account-wide settings in your privacy dashboard ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/privacy?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Logging-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )
* Enabling Zero Data Retention (ZDR ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/zdr?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Logging-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )) for enterprise-grade privacy ( https://openrouter.ai/enterprise?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Logging-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Want to support model improvements? You may opt into prompt logging for a 1% usage discount ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/privacy?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Logging

        # Get API key

        # Look for API key pattern

        # Navigate to dashboard

import requests, re

s = requests.Session()
r = s.get('https://www.alphavantage.co/support/#api-key')

# Find CSRF and form action
csrf = re.findall(r'csrfmiddlewaretoken.*?value=["\']([^"\']+)["\']', r.text)
print("CSRF tokens:", csrf)

# Find form
forms = re.findall(r'<form[^>]*action=["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*>', r.text)
print("Forms:", forms)

# Find all input names
inputs = re.findall(r'<input[^>]*name=["\']([^"\']+)["\']', r.text)
print("Inputs:", inputs)

if csrf:
    data = {
        'csrfmiddlewaretoken': csrf[0],
        'first_text': 'Bull',
        'last_text': 'Sage',
        'occupation_text': 'Trader',
        'organization_text': 'BullSage Trader',
        'email_text': 'bullsagetrader@gmail.com'
    }
    r2 = s.post('https://www.alphavantage.co/create_post_request/demo/', data=data, 
                headers={'Referer': 'https://www.alphavantage.co/support/'})

    # Look for API key in response

from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
import re, time

with sync_playwright() as p:
    browser = p.chromium.launch(
        headless=False,
        args=['--no-sandbox', '--disable-gpu']
    )
    page = browser.new_page()
    
    ##### 1. ALPHA VANTAGE - Claim API Key #####
    print("=== ALPHA VANTAGE ===")
    page.goto('https://www.alphavantage.co/support/#api-key')
    page.wait_for_load_state('networkidle')
    
    # Fill the form
    page.select_option('select', label='Investor') if page.query_selector('select') else None
    
    # Find and fill fields
    inputs = page.query_selector_all('input[type="text"], input[type="email"]')
    for inp in inputs:
        name = inp.get_attribute('name') or ''
        placeholder = inp.get_attribute('placeholder') or ''
        print(f"  Field: name={name} placeholder={placeholder}")
    
    page.fill('input[name="first"]', 'Bull')
    page.fill('input[name="last"]', 'Sage')
    page.fill('input[name="organization"]', 'BullSage Trader')
    page.fill('input[name="email"]', 'bullsagetrader@gmail.com')
    
    # Submit
    submit = page.query_selector('button[type="submit"], input[type="submit"], .btn-primary')
    if submit:
        submit.click()
        page.wait_for_timeout(5000)
        
        # Check for API key in response
        content = page.content()
        keys = re.findall(r'\b[A-Z0-9]{12,20}\b', content)
        print(f"  Keys found: {list(set(keys))[:10]}")
        
        # Look for key display
        text = page.inner_text('body')
        if 'key' in text.lower():
            for line in text.split('\n'):
                if 'key' in line.lower() and len(line) < 200:
                    print(f"  KEY LINE: {line.strip()}")
    
    browser.close()
    print("Done")

import json, websocket, time, requests, re

tabs = requests.get('http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/list').json()
ws_url = [t['webSocketDebuggerUrl'] for t in tabs if t['type'] == 'page'][0]
ws = websocket.create_connection(ws_url)
msg_id = 0

def cdp(method, params=None):
    global msg_id
    msg_id += 1
    msg = {"id": msg_id, "method": method}
    if params: msg["params"] = params
    ws.send(json.dumps(msg))
    while True:
        resp = json.loads(ws.recv())
        if resp.get("id") == msg_id:
            return resp

def js(expr):
    r = cdp("Runtime.evaluate", {"expression": expr, "returnByValue": True})
    return r.get("result", {}).get("result", {}).get("value")

arguments.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, websocket, time, requests

tabs = requests.get('http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/list').json()
ws_url = [t['webSocketDebuggerUrl'] for t in tabs if t['type'] == 'page'][0]
ws = websocket.create_connection(ws_url)
msg_id = 0

def cdp(method, params=None):
    global msg_id; msg_id += 1
    msg = {"id": msg_id, "method": method}
    if params: msg["params"] = params
    ws.send(json.dumps(msg))
    while True:
        resp = json.loads(ws.recv())
        if resp.get("id") == msg_id: return resp

def js(expr):
    r = cdp("Runtime.evaluate", {"expression": expr, "returnByValue": True, "awaitPromise": True})
    return r.get("result", {}).get("result", {}).get("value")

# Navigate to internal integration form
cdp("Page.navigate", {"url": "https://www.notion.so/profile/integrations/internal/form/new-integration"})
time.sleep(4)

# Type integration name
result = cdp("DOM.getDocument")
doc = result['result']['root']['nodeId']
inp_node = cdp("DOM.querySelector", {"nodeId": doc, "selector": "input[type='text']"})
cdp("DOM.focus", {"nodeId": inp_node['result']['nodeId']})
time.sleep(0.3)

for char in "BullSage Trader":
    cdp("Input.dispatchKeyEvent", {"type": "keyDown", "text": char})
    cdp("Input.dispatchKeyEvent", {"type": "keyUp", "text": char})

time.sleep(0.5)
val = js("document.querySelector('input[type=\"text\"]').value")
print("Name:", val)

# Check if workspace selector needs to be set
selects = js("JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('select')).map(e => ({options: Array.from(e.options).map(o => o.text)})))")
print("Selects:", selects)

# Click Create
js("document.querySelectorAll('button, [role=\"button\"]').forEach(b => { if(b.innerText.trim() === 'Create' || b.innerText.trim() === 'Créer') b.click(); })")
time.sleep(5)

url = js("window.location.href")
print("URL:", url)
text = js("document.body.innerText.substring(0, 1500)")
print("After create:", text[:1200])

ws.close()

# Internal integrations can't create workspace-level pages. 
# Need to connect an existing page first from the Notion UI, then create under it.
# Let me do it via the browser
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, websocket, time, requests

tabs = requests.get('http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/list').json()
ws_url = [t['webSocketDebuggerUrl'] for t in tabs if t['type'] == 'page'][0]
ws = websocket.create_connection(ws_url)
msg_id = 0

def cdp(method, params=None):
    global msg_id; msg_id += 1
    msg = {"id": msg_id, "method": method}
    if params: msg["params"] = params
    ws.send(json.dumps(msg))
    while True:
        resp = json.loads(ws.recv())
        if resp.get("id") == msg_id: return resp

def js(expr):
    r = cdp("Runtime.evaluate", {"expression": expr, "returnByValue": True, "awaitPromise": True})
    return r.get("result", {}).get("result", {}).get("value")

# Go to the Trading Hub page and connect the integration there
cdp("Page.navigate", {"url": "https://www.notion.so"})
time.sleep(5)

# Get all page links from sidebar
links = js("""
JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a[href*="notion.so"]')).map(a => ({
    text: a.innerText?.substring(0, 50),
    href: a.href
})).filter(a => a.text && a.text.trim()))
""")
print("Sidebar links:", links)

ws.close()
PYEOF
